rationale: Live-action UGC of a guy cheering about reaching "level 55" badly mismatches Helix Jump's identity: the game's pull is the immediate visceral satisfaction of the bouncing ball + neon tower smash, which UGC reaction shots completely hide — you'd be selling vibes instead of the loop. The muted blue/silver/black UGC palette also fights the game's high-contrast neon look, and Helix Jump has no meaningful "level 55" progression milestone to anchor the bragging hook. Audience skew (broad casual) is the only real overlap; if tested, force gameplay-on-tablet to dominate the frame and swap the brag for a "can't stop bouncing" near-miss reaction.
